name: Vector clock for causal ordering
task: Create /tmp/eval_vc/vc.py with class VectorClock(node_id). Methods: tick() increments local counter; merge(other_clock) merges another clock dict (taking pairwise max) and ticks; snapshot() returns a copy of the clock dict. Class method (or static) compare(a, b) over two clock dicts returns 'before', 'after', 'concurrent', or 'equal'.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_vc/vc.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); a = mod.VectorClock('A'); b = mod.VectorClock('B'); a.tick(); a.tick(); b.tick(); b.merge(a.snapshot()); assert mod.VectorClock.compare({'A':1},{'A':2}) == 'before'; assert mod.VectorClock.compare({'A':2},{'A':1}) == 'after'; assert mod.VectorClock.compare({'A':1,'B':0},{'A':0,'B':1}) == 'concurrent'; assert mod.VectorClock.compare({'A':1},{'A':1}) == 'equal'; print('OK')
